name: AT2018cow Frontier-Science LLM Benchmark
description: A seven-dimension benchmark for evaluating frontier scientific reasoning in large language models using the astrophysical transient AT2018cow. The benchmark includes literature discovery, literature synthesis, claim verification, hypothesis discrimination, formal scientific reasoning, hypothesis generation, and frontier open-question reasoning.
citation: Margutti et al. 2019, An Embedded X-Ray Source Shines through the Aspherical AT 2018cow
Kuin et al. 2019, Swift spectra of AT2018cow: a white dwarf tidal disruption event?
Tsuna and Lu 2025, Stellar tidal disruptions by newborn neutron stars or black holes
Metzger 2022, Luminous Fast Blue Optical Transients and Type Ibn/Icn SNe from Wolf-Rayet/Black Hole Mergers
rai:dataCollection: The benchmark was constructed from expert-designed scientific reasoning tasks centered on AT2018cow. It contains prompts, gold-answer expectations, scoring rubrics, hallucination cap rules, model score tables, and evaluation scripts. It does not redistribute full copyrighted paper texts.
rai:dataCollectionType: Document analysis; manual human curator; expert benchmark design; evaluation rubric construction.
rai:dataCollectionRawData: Source materials include published papers and public scientific knowledge about AT2018cow and related luminous fast blue optical transients. The package includes references, prompts, rubrics, and metadata rather than full paper excerpts.
rai:dataUseCases: Evaluation and benchmarking of LLMs for frontier scientific reasoning.
Analysis of model strengths and weaknesses across literature discovery, synthesis, verification, hypothesis discrimination, formal reasoning, hypothesis generation, and open-question reasoning.
Development of multi-agent AI systems for AI-assisted scientific discovery.
rai:dataLimitations: The benchmark is centered on one astrophysical case study and should be extended to additional domains before broad generalization.
Some scoring requires domain-expert judgment, especially for frontier open questions and hypothesis generation.
The package does not include full copyrighted paper texts; users must obtain source literature independently.
Single-run LLM evaluations may be sensitive to prompting and model version changes.
rai:dataBiases: The benchmark emphasizes astrophysical transients and may overrepresent reasoning patterns specific to observational astrophysics.
Gold answers and scoring rubrics reflect expert interpretation of a still-open scientific problem.
Model comparisons may depend on access mode, date, and proprietary model behavior.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No human-subject, personally identifiable, or sensitive personal information is included.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Positive intended impact: improve evaluation of AI systems for scientific discovery and encourage evidence-grounded, uncertainty-aware reasoning. Potential risks: overreliance on LLM outputs in scientific decision-making; mitigated by expert review, hallucination caps, and explicit uncertainty requirements.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: Versioned releases will update prompts, scoring rubrics, and example tasks as the benchmark expands to additional scientific domains. Major changes should increment the version number and document differences.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Tasks were manually structured into seven dimensions. Raw qualitative outputs are scored using dimension-specific rubrics and converted to percentages. Hallucination and plausibility caps are applied after raw score conversion.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Gold-answer expectations and scoring criteria are expert-designed. Final scoring should involve domain expert review for scientific correctness and uncertainty assessment.

distribution.name: tasks.json
distribution.description: Full benchmark task definitions for D1-D7.

distribution.name: scoring_config.json
distribution.description: Scoring rubrics, raw score ranges, rescoring rules, and hallucination cap rules.

distribution.name: score_table.json
distribution.description: Reported model performance scores from the paper.

distribution.name: submission_template.json
distribution.description: Template for recording model outputs and evaluator scores.
